mission.title: Install and Configure Stacker on Kubernetes
mission.description: Stacker is a tool for building OCI images natively using a declarative YAML format. Installing Stacker allows you to create container images in a vendor-neutral manner, leveraging its rootless and hermetically sealed build capabilities.

steps.title: Install stacker
steps.description:
Download stacker from GitHub releases:
```bash
curl -Lo stacker https://github.com/project-stacker/stacker/releases/latest/download/stacker
chmod +x stacker
sudo mv stacker /usr/local/bin/
```
See https://github.com/project-stacker/stacker/releases for specific versions.

steps.title: Create a stacker.yaml file
steps.description:
Create a `stacker.yaml` that defines your OCI image:
```yaml
my-image:
  from:
    type: docker
    url: docker://ubuntu:22.04
  run: |
    apt-get update
    apt-get install -y nginx
```

steps.title: Build the OCI image
steps.description:
Build the image using stacker:
```bash
stacker build
```
The resulting OCI image will be stored in the local OCI layout.

resolution.summary: Stacker is installed as a CLI tool for building OCI images natively via declarative YAML.

upgrade.title: Download latest stacker release
upgrade.description:
```bash
curl -Lo stacker https://github.com/project-stacker/stacker/releases/latest/download/stacker
chmod +x stacker
sudo mv stacker /usr/local/bin/
```

troubleshooting.title: Permission denied during build
troubleshooting.description:
Stacker supports rootless builds. Ensure user namespaces are enabled:
```bash
sysctl kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone
```

troubleshooting.title: LXC dependency missing
troubleshooting.description:
Stacker uses LXC for hermetic builds. Install LXC:
```bash
sudo apt-get install -y lxc-utils
```

troubleshooting.title: Build cache issues
troubleshooting.description:
Clear the build cache:
```bash
stacker clean
```

prerequisites.description: Linux is required for stacker builds (uses LXC containers). No Kubernetes cluster needed.
